message.abstract:
<jats:title>ABSTRACT</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  The pandemic of the coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) has created the largest global health crisis in almost a century. Following exposure to SARS-CoV-2, the virus particles replicate in the lungs, induce a “cytokine storm” and potentially cause life-threatening inflammatory disease. Low frequencies of function SARS-CoV-2-specific CD4
                  <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                  and CD8
                  <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                  T cells in the lungs of COVID-19 patients were associated with severe cases of COVID-19. The apparent low level of T cell-attracting CXCL9, CXCL10, and CXCL11 chemokines in infected lungs may not be sufficient enough to assure the sequestration and/or homing of CD4
                  <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                  and CD8
                  <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                  T cells from the circulation into infected lungs. We hypothesize that a Coronavirus vaccine strategy that boosts the frequencies of functional SARS-CoV-2-specific CD4
                  <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                  and CD8
                  <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                  T cells in the lungs would lead to better protection against SARS-CoV-2 infection, COVID19-like symptoms, and death. In the present study, we designed and pre-clinically tested the safety, immunogenicity, and protective efficacy of a novel multi-epitope//CXCL11 prime/pull mucosal Coronavirus vaccine. This prime/pull vaccine strategy consists of intranasal delivery of a lung-tropic adeno-associated virus type 9 (AAV-9) vector that incorporates highly conserved human B, CD4
                  <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                  CD8
                  <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                  cell epitopes of SARS-CoV-2 (
                  <jats:italic>prime</jats:italic>
                  ) and pulling the primed B and T cells into the lungs using the T cell attracting chemokine, CXCL-11 (
                  <jats:italic>pull</jats:italic>
                  ). We demonstrated that immunization of HLA-DR*0101/HLA-A*0201/hACE2 triple transgenic mice with this multi-epitope//CXCL11 prime/pull Coronavirus mucosal vaccine: (
                  <jats:italic>i</jats:italic>
                  ) Increased the frequencies of CD4
                  <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                  and CD8
                  <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                  T
                  <jats:sub>EM</jats:sub>
                  , T
                  <jats:sub>CM</jats:sub>
                  , and T
                  <jats:sub>RM</jats:sub>
                  cells in the lungs; and (
                  <jats:italic>ii</jats:italic>
                  ) reduced COVID19-like symptoms, lowered virus replication, and prevented deaths following challenge with SARS-CoV-2. These findings discuss the importance of bolstering the number and function of lung-resident memory CD4
                  <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                  and CD8
                  <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                  T cells for better protection against SARS-CoV-2 infection, COVID-19-like symptoms, and death.
                </jats:p>
title:
A Multi-Epitope/CXCL11 Prime/Pull Coronavirus Mucosal Vaccine Boosts the Frequency and the Function of Lung-Resident CD4
                  <sup>+</sup>
                  and CD8
                  <sup>+</sup>
                  Memory T Cells and Protects Against COVID-19-like Symptoms and Death Caused by SARS-CoV-2 infection
